narms_isolates.long_name: Number of isolates tested by NARMS
narms_isolates.short_description: Monthly count of bacterial isolates submitted for antimicrobial resistance testing
narms_isolates.long_description: Number of bacterial isolates from human, animal, and food specimens submitted to NARMS for antimicrobial susceptibility testing. Data from the BEAM Dashboard Report.
narms_isolates.statement: In {location}, {value} isolates were submitted to NARMS

narms_pct_amr.long_name: Percentage of isolates with clinically important antimicrobial resistance
narms_pct_amr.short_description: Percentage of tested isolates showing clinically important antimicrobial resistance
narms_pct_amr.long_description: Percentage of isolates with clinically important antimicrobial resistance, as reported in the BEAM Dashboard. Source: CDC NARMS BEAM Dashboard Report Data.
narms_pct_amr.statement: In {location}, {value}% of isolates showed clinically important AMR

narms_now_pct_resistant.long_name: Percentage of isolates resistant to a specific antimicrobial agent
narms_now_pct_resistant.short_description: Percentage of tested human clinical isolates showing resistance to a specific antimicrobial agent or matching a specific resistance pattern
narms_now_pct_resistant.long_description: Percentage of human clinical isolates tested by NARMS showing resistance to a specific antimicrobial agent or matching a specific resistance pattern, as determined by AST (antimicrobial susceptibility testing) or WGS (whole genome sequencing). Resistance is based on CLSI breakpoints. Data extracted from the NARMS Now interactive dashboard.
narms_now_pct_resistant.statement: In {location}, {value}% of {species} isolates were resistant to {agent}

narms_now_n_resistant.long_name: Number of isolates resistant to a specific antimicrobial agent
narms_now_n_resistant.short_description: Count of human clinical isolates showing resistance to a specific antimicrobial
narms_now_n_resistant.long_description: Number of human clinical isolates tested by NARMS showing resistance to a specific antimicrobial agent. Numerator of the resistance percentage.
narms_now_n_resistant.statement: In {location}, {value} isolates of {species} were resistant to {agent}

narms_now_n_tested.long_name: Number of isolates tested for antimicrobial susceptibility
narms_now_n_tested.short_description: Total number of human clinical isolates tested for resistance to a specific antimicrobial
narms_now_n_tested.long_description: Total number of human clinical isolates tested by NARMS for susceptibility to a specific antimicrobial agent. Denominator of the resistance percentage.
narms_now_n_tested.statement: In {location}, {value} isolates of {species} were tested for {agent}

narms_beam.name: NARMS BEAM Dashboard Report Data
narms_beam.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
narms_beam.description: Monthly counts of bacterial isolates and antimicrobial resistance rates from the NARMS Bacterial Enteric Antimicrobial Monitoring (BEAM) Dashboard. Covers Salmonella, Campylobacter, Shigella, STEC, and Vibrio from human, animal, and food specimens.
narms_beam.restrictions: Public domain. CDC data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.

narms_now.name: NARMS Now: Human Data - Antimicrobial Resistance
narms_now.location: NARMS Now Interactive Dashboard - Human Data
narms_now.organization: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
narms_now.description: Detailed antimicrobial resistance data from NARMS human clinical isolates, including resistance by individual antimicrobial agent (with CLSI class) and by resistance pattern (multidrug resistance profiles). Data available for 30 species/serotypes across 5 genera (Campylobacter, E. coli O157, Non-cholera Vibrio, Salmonella, Shigella). Both AST (phenotypic) and WGS (genotypic) test method results are available. Data extracted from the NARMS Now Power BI dashboard. Suggested citation: CDC. National Antimicrobial Resistance Monitoring System (NARMS) Now: Human Data. Atlanta, Georgia: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services.
narms_now.restrictions: Public domain. CDC data is generally not subject to copyright restrictions.
